title: Relate Salesforce Reports and Dashboards to Metrics
story: As a data analyst, I want to associate Salesforce reports and dashboards with specific metrics, so that I can quickly find the business insights related to each metric.
description:
Enables users to relate Salesforce reports and dashboards to metrics in the Metrics Glossary. The system queries Salesforce to fetch report and dashboard metadata using SOQL queries, stores the results in local database tables, and provides a UI within the metric detail panel to search, browse, and assign reports or dashboards to individual metrics.

The data model includes:
- A Reports table storing Salesforce report metadata (name, ID, folder, dates, etc.)
- A Dashboards table storing Salesforce dashboard metadata (title, ID, folder, dates, etc.)
- A mapping/junction table relating metrics to reports and dashboards (many-to-many)

Reports and dashboards are fetched from Salesforce via SOQL queryAll and cached locally. A refresh button on each tab allows users to re-sync metadata from Salesforce on demand. Users assign reports and dashboards from the metric detail offcanvas panel via dedicated tabs.
acceptance_criteria: System fetches Salesforce report metadata via SOQL and stores locally in a Reports table
System fetches Salesforce dashboard metadata via SOQL and stores locally in a Dashboards table
Metric detail panel includes a Reports tab and a Dashboards tab
User can search and browse reports/dashboards within each tab
User can assign reports and dashboards to a metric from the respective tabs
One metric can be related to multiple reports and multiple dashboards
A refresh icon on each tab re-syncs metadata from Salesforce
Reports and dashboards are searchable by name within the assignment interface
ux_changes: Adds Reports and Dashboards tabs to the metric detail offcanvas panel. Each tab shows a searchable, sortable table of available items with click-to-assign. A refresh icon on each tab triggers a Salesforce re-sync.
middleware_changes: New service layer for Salesforce metadata sync — queries Salesforce SOQL endpoints for Report and Dashboard objects, upserts into local tables. Assignment service manages the many-to-many relationship between metrics and reports/dashboards.
data_model_changes: New Reports table (CreatedById, Description, DeveloperName, FolderName, Format, etc.), new Dashboards table (Title, Description, DeveloperName, FolderId, FolderName, etc.), and a junction/mapping table for metric-to-report and metric-to-dashboard relationships.
api_changes: Endpoints for Salesforce report/dashboard sync, report/dashboard list retrieval, and metric-report/dashboard assignment CRUD.
implementation_notes: Initial Salesforce Analytics REST API endpoints returned empty arrays. Switched to SOQL queryAll against the Report and Dashboard objects, which returned full metadata. Dashboard-to-report relationships exist in Salesforce but are not captured at this phase — a future junction table will model this. Reports and dashboards are stored locally to avoid repeated Salesforce API calls.
comment_summary: Extensive back-and-forth resolving confusion between this ticket (relating reports/dashboards to metrics on the metrics tab) and MBT-205 (a standalone reports/dashboards page). SOQL queries replaced the initial Analytics API approach after empty results. Developer added refresh functionality and a local caching strategy. Assignment UI completed and pushed to staging.
